title: Phymatopsis oxyloba

taxonomy: Phymatopsis oxyloba belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida and is part of the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant is a type of club moss, a group of vascular plants that are known for their simple, needle-like leaves and reproductive structures. Phymatopsis oxyloba is a genus of extinct lycopods, with fossil records dating back to the Devonian period. The classification of Phymatopsis oxyloba is further divided into the order Lycopodiales and the division Lycopodiophyta. The specific classification is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new evidence emerges.
